title: azure-rate-limit-deepseek-spread
description:
This is a continuation of azure-rate-limit-dev-run. Since the prior concern at 2026-05-30T03:05:26.666171+00:00, the same provider-side Azure quota/rate-limit failure has continued and broadened within this window. Three separate persona runs on azure/deepseek-v4-pro failed across stories 27, 16, and 17 with the same explicit error text: `Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0.` The failures span both `dev` and `test_implementer`, which points away from a single story-domain defect and toward a shared provider or model-access constraint.

The immediate downstream impact is stalled work on multiple stories: the failed runs consumed wall-clock time of about 187s, 403s, and 737s respectively before terminating with zero tokens processed and zero cost recorded. There is no in-window retry storm on any single (story, persona) pair, but the cross-story spread strengthens the case that this is a sustained factory-external or infrastructure-adjacent limit rather than an isolated transient. Other detectors in this bundle are quiet, so the current concern is concentrated on provider availability/capacity for the deepseek-v4-pro path.

A secondary signal is worktree drift: watcher notes report missing-ID worktrees growing from two to five, with new worktree creates for stories 72, 57, and 54 visible in the underlying git stream. The available evidence does not show whether that drift is causally related to the Azure failures, so it should be treated as a separate continuity note rather than the primary incident pattern.

evidence.summary_excerpt: The same Azure quota/rate-limit failure escalated in the prior note continued this minute and broadened: three deepseek-v4-pro persona runs failed across stories 27, 16, and 17 with the same diagnostically clear token-limit error.

evidence.excerpt: persona=test_implementer model=azure/deepseek-v4-pro success=false duration_s=187.05 error="...RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0."
persona=dev model=azure/deepseek-v4-pro success=false duration_s=737.292 error="...RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0."
persona=dev model=azure/deepseek-v4-pro success=false duration_s=403.438 error="...RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0."

{"noteworthy":"Three failed runs all show the same clear infrastructure-side Azure RateLimitError/quota token-cap message across stories 27, 16, and 17; this continues the pattern escalated in the prior note."}
{"noteworthy":"No per-(story, persona) retry storm in-window, but failures are distributed across three separate story/persona groups, which strengthens the provider-side pattern."}

{"noteworthy":"Worktree drift increased to five missing-ID directories (52, 54, 55, 57, 72), up from two in the prior note; likely cleanup lag or DB/worktree mismatch, worth watching but secondary to the quota failures."}

escalation_reason: Azure deepseek-v4-pro quota/rate-limit failures have persisted across two watcher-note intervals and now span three runs on three stories (stories 27, 16, and 17) over at least the last several minutes, with clear provider-side error text and blocked story progress. L3 judgment is needed because the pattern is diagnostically clear, broadening, and attributable to shared model/provider access rather than a single story bug.
